tate of CaiifoTl1ia 
Fair Political Practices CommissioIl. 
P.o. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
107 SOUTH BROADWAY. •• 
SUITE 7007 
••• 
LOS ANGELES,90012 
Mr. Dick Bryant 
Los Angeles Registrar-Recorder 
5557 Ferguson Drive 
Los Angeles, California 90022 
Dear Mr. Bryant: 
October 5, 1981 
This letter is in response to your request made last Friday, 
October 2nd concerning the filing obligations of a major donor 
committee. 
You asked what filing obligation does a Major donor incur if 
during the first threshold period a person has contributions 
totaling $4,500, and, during the second threshold period makes 
additional contributions totaling $500 for a cumulative amount of 
$5,000, thereby qualifying the person as a Major donor pursuant to 
Government Code Section 82013(c)? 
You also asked what the filing obligation would be if an 
individual, during the first threshold period had made a $4,700 
in contributions, and during the second threshold Deriod had Made 
additional contributions less than $500 but cumulative to the 
contributions in the first threshold period of $5,000 or more 
again qualifying the person as a major donor committee? 
In the first example you gave, I advised that the person 
qualified as a major donor committee in the second threshold period 
since the total cumulative amount of contributions made was $5,000. 
In addition, since the contributions made during the second 
threshold period were $500, the major donor would be required to 
file the second threshold statement pursuant to Government Code 
Section 84200(b)(2). 
For the second example you cited, since the contributions made 
during the second threshold period were less than $500 and there 
were no other expenditures by the person, the person likewise 
qualified as a major donor committee since the cumulative amount 
of contributions made was $5,000. 
The major donor is required to 
file the semi-annual campaign statement pursuant to Government 
Code Section 84200(a) since the level of activity was less than 
$500 for the specified threshold period. 
SACRAMENTO (916) 322·5660 
• 
LOS ANGELES (213) 620-5196

sections.question: You asked what filing obligation does a Major donor incur if during the first threshold period a person has contributions totaling $4,500, and, during the second threshold period makes additional contributions totaling $500 for a cumulative amount of $5,000, thereby qualifying the person as a Major donor pursuant to Government Code Section 82013(c)? You also asked what the filing obligation would be if an individual, during the first threshold period had made a $4,700 in contributions, and during the second threshold period had made additional contributions less than $500 but cumulative to the contributions in the first threshold period of $5,000 or more again qualifying the person as a major donor committee?
sections.conclusion: In the first example, the person qualified as a major donor committee in the second threshold period since the total cumulative amount of contributions made was $5,000, and must file the second threshold statement pursuant to Government Code Section 84200(b)(2) since contributions in the second period were $500. In the second example, since contributions in the second threshold period were less than $500 with no other expenditures, the person qualified as a major donor committee and is required to file the semi-annual campaign statement pursuant to Government Code Section 84200(a).
sections.question_synthetic: What are the filing obligations for a major donor committee when contributions across two threshold periods cumulatively reach $5,000, and does the filing requirement differ based on whether the contributions in the second threshold period meet or fall below $500?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A person qualifies as a major donor when cumulative contributions reach $5,000 across threshold periods, with filing obligations determined by the activity level in the second threshold period: if $500 or more, file the second threshold statement; if less than $500, file the semi-annual campaign statement.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document dated October 5, 1981, addressed to Los Angeles Registrar-Recorder. Contains clear question and conclusion sections with specific statutory references to Government Code sections 82013(c), 84200(b)(2), and 84200(a). Minor OCR artifacts present but do not significantly impact understanding.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You asked what filing obligation does a Major donor incur if during the first threshold period a person has contributions totaling $4,500, and, during the second threshold period makes additional contributions totaling $500 for a cumulative amount of $5,000, thereby qualifying the person as a Major donor pursuant to Government Code Section 82013(c)? You also asked what the filing obligation would be if an individual, during the first threshold period had made a $4,700 in contributions, and during the second threshold period had made additional contributions less than $500 but cumulative to the contributions in the first threshold period of $5,000 or more again qualifying the person as a major donor committee?

CONCLUSION: In the first example, the person qualified as a major donor committee in the second threshold period since the total cumulative amount of contributions made was $5,000, and must file the second threshold statement pursuant to Government Code Section 84200(b)(2) since contributions in the second period were $500. In the second example, since contributions in the second threshold period were less than $500 with no other expenditures, the person qualified as a major donor committee and is required to file the semi-annual campaign statement pursuant to Government Code Section 84200(a).

embedding.summary: FPPC provides advice on major donor committee filing obligations under two scenarios involving cumulative contributions of $5,000 across multiple threshold periods, distinguishing between filing requirements based on contribution amounts in the second threshold period.
